Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Amended Responses To Interrogatories, And For Sanctions; Statement Of Interrogatories And Responses
Off Calendar Aug-20-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Enrique Pearce, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Ralph Martin
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Elizabeth Voger, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: James Knauss

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiff's motion to compel a further answer to form interrogatory 16.1 is granted. Defendant is ordered to serve a supplemental verified response that responds to sub parts (a) and (c) on or before August 22, 2007. The Court finds that defendant made evasive responses to interrogatory 16.1. Defendant responded that someone other than either defendant or plaintiff contributed to the occurrence but failed to identify the person or persons. In addition, defendant failed to identify persons with knowledge of the relevant facts. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1140 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
MODIFICATION: To clarify that response is for Sub B + C

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Enrique Pearce, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Ralph Martin
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Elizabeth Voger, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: James Knauss

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Ralph Martin's motion to compel further answers to interrogatories is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the supplemental response but notes that many of the answers remain non-responsive. The Court finds that defendant failed to respond in a timely fashion and made evasive responses thereby requiring the filing of the instant motion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $790 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the supplemental responses. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
